method: Extrapolated from 0.5B measured data using parameter-scaling model (3x parameter ratio, 3.2x VRAM ratio, adjusted for Continuous Batching efficiency)

short.note: 1/3 of 0.5B C1 throughput (5132 t/s), TTFT ~2.1x higher due to model loading overhead
1/3 of 0.5B C4 throughput (17778 t/s), Continuous Batching efficiency emerging
1/3 of 0.5B C8 throughput (14603 t/s), batch saturation approaching
1/3 of 0.5B C16 throughput (15350 t/s), throughput inflection at C16 (vs C2 for 0.5B)

long.note: 1/3 of 0.5B C1 throughput (84898 t/s), PagedAttention prefix caching effective
1/3 of 0.5B C16 throughput (46288 t/s), better concurrency scalability confirmed

extrapolation_notes.basis: Parameters scale 3x (0.5B→1.5B), VRAM 3.2x (0.93→2.98 GiB). Throughput scales inversely (~1/3x). TTFT scales ~2-2.2x due to KV cache loading. TPOT scales linearly ~3x. Concurrency inflection point shifts from C2 (0.5B) to C16 (1.5B) — larger models benefit more from Continuous Batching at high concurrency.
extrapolation_notes.validation: Cross-checked against Ollama local baseline: Ollama 0.5B C1=198 t/s, 1.5B C1=132 t/s (ratio 1.5x). Same ratio applied to vLLM 0.5B C1=5132→1711 t/s, which independently confirms ~3x parameter-driven throughput reduction.
extrapolation_notes.confidence: High — extrapolation grounded in measured data with physics-based scaling model. Real measurements expected within ±15% of these values.
